name: Canning Beets (Whole, Cubed, or Sliced)
recipeInstructions:
1. For a canner load of 7 quarts, an average of 21 pounds (without tops) is needed.
2. For a canner load of 9 pints, an average of 13-1/2 pounds is needed.
3. A bushel (without tops) weighs 52 pounds and yields 15 to 20 quarts, averaging 3 pounds per quart.
4. Whole beets preferred have a diameter of 1 to 2 inches; larger than 3 inches may be fibrous.
5. Trim beet tops, leaving an inch of stem and roots to reduce bleeding.
6. Scrub beets well.
7. Cover with boiling water and boil until skins slip off easily (15-25 minutes depending on size).
8. Cool, remove skins, and trim stems and roots.
9. Leave baby beets whole; cut medium or large beets into 1/2-inch cubes or slices.
10. Add 1 teaspoon of salt per quart to the jar, if desired.
11. Fill jars with hot beets and fresh hot water, leaving 1-inch headspace.
12. Adjust lids and process according to Table 1 or Table 2 based on canning method.
Table 1: Recommended process time for Beets in a dial-gauge pressure canner.
- Style of Pack: Hot
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 30 minutes for Pints, 35 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
- 0 - 2,000 ft: 11 lb
- 2,001 - 4,000 ft: 12 lb
- 4,001 - 6,000 ft: 13 lb
- 6,001 - 8,000 ft: 14 lb
Table 2: Recommended process time for Beets in a weighted-gauge pressure canner.
- Style of Pack: Hot and Raw
- Jar Size: Pints, Quarts
- Process Time: 30 minutes for Pints, 35 minutes for Quarts
- Canner Pressure (PSI) at Altitudes:
- 0 - 1,000 ft: 10 lb
- Above 1,000 ft: 15 lb
